title: rhinanthus aristatus subsp. gracilis
intro: Rhinanthus aristatus subsp. gracilis, also known as the bearded broomrape or yellow-broomrape, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. This hemiparasitic plant is known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of iridoid glycosides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rhinanthus aristatus subsp. gracilis include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of various conditions, such as gastrointestinal issues and respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy in the form of infusions or tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
